heading: What is current electricity?
definition: Current electricity is the continuous flow of electric charge through a conductor. It requires a closed circuit and a source of electromotive force (EMF) such as a battery to maintain the flow of charge.

concepts.title: Ohm's Law
concepts.description: The current through a conductor is directly proportional to the voltage across it (at constant temperature).

concepts.title: Series & Parallel Circuits
concepts.description: Components in series share the same current; components in parallel share the same voltage.

concepts.title: EMF & Internal Resistance
concepts.description: A real battery has internal resistance that reduces the terminal voltage under load.

formula: V = IR  |  P = IV  |  V = EMF - Ir
variableDefs:
V = potential difference (Volts)
I = current (Amperes)
R = resistance (Ohms)
EMF = electromotive force (Volts)
r = internal resistance (Ω)
